title: How to Launch a Website From Blank in GoHighLevel — Fast
description: Launch a professional website from blank in GoHighLevel—fast. Step-by-step guide from planning to publishing your first site in hours, not weeks.
html_content:
Launching a website from scratch doesn't have to be complicated. Most business owners and agency owners get stuck because they're juggling too many tools—a website builder here, a CRM there, email marketing somewhere else. That friction kills momentum.

GoHighLevel changes that equation. Its integrated website builder lets you go from blank page to published site in hours, not weeks. You're not switching between platforms. You're not learning five different interfaces. Everything lives in one place—your website, your CRM, your marketing automation, your client management. In this guide, I'll walk you through launching a professional website from blank in GoHighLevel, step by step, so you can get live fast.

And if you want to test this yourself risk-free, start a free 30-day GoHighLevel trial here—that's double the standard trial, no credit card needed.

📋 In This Guide

  • Preparation & Planning: Before You Build
  • How to Create a Blank Website in GoHighLevel
  • Build and Design Your Home Page
  • Adding Pages, Navigation, and Site Structure
  • Publishing Your Website and Going Live

Preparation & Planning: Before You Build

The biggest mistake I see is people opening GoHighLevel and immediately hitting "create website" without a plan. They end up redesigning three times because they didn't think through structure first.

Before you touch the builder, answer these questions:

  • What's your primary goal? Lead generation? Sales? Information? Your goal shapes everything—layout, messaging, CTAs.
  • Who's your audience? What problem are you solving for them? This drives your copy and design direction.
  • What pages do you need? Home, About, Services, Contact—or something different? Map this out on paper or a document.
  • What's your brand voice? Professional? Casual? Creative? Your copy and design tone need to align.
  • Do you have brand assets? Logo, color palette, fonts? Gather these before you start so you're not hunting for them mid-build.

This prep phase saves hours. You're not second-guessing decisions mid-build. You know what you're building and why.

💡 Pro Tip

Write your home page headline and primary CTA before you open GoHighLevel. This clarity makes the design phase faster and keeps you focused on conversion, not just aesthetics.

How to Create a Blank Website in GoHighLevel

Once you've planned, creating the blank site takes three minutes.

Step 1: Navigate to Websites Log into your GoHighLevel account. From your dashboard, find the Websites section in the left sidebar menu. Click it.

Step 2: Start a New Website Click the + Create New or New Website button. You'll see two options: From Blank or From Template. Select From Blank. This gives you a clean canvas with no pre-built sections cluttering your design.

Step 3: Name Your Website Give your website a name. This can be your business name or the domain you plan to use. This name appears in your workspace, not on the actual site. Hit Create.

GoHighLevel generates a blank page with a default header and footer. You're now in the drag-and-drop editor. This is where the fun starts.

The interface is clean: on the left sidebar, you'll see design elements (sections, buttons, forms, text blocks). In the center, your canvas. On the right, properties and settings for whatever element you've selected. If you've used page builders before, this will feel familiar. If not, don't worry—it's intuitive.

Double the standard trial. No credit card required to start.

Start My Free Trial →

Build and Design Your Home Page

Your home page is the critical page. It's where first impressions happen. In GoHighLevel's blank template, you start with a basic header and footer. Everything in between is yours to build.

Add Your Hero Section Start with a hero section—the large banner at the top with a headline and CTA. Click Add Section and choose Hero or Text + Image. Replace the placeholder text with your headline and supporting copy. This is the promise you make to visitors.

Customize Colors and Fonts In the right sidebar, you'll see styling options. Set your brand colors (background, text, button). Keep it consistent—use your brand palette, not GoHighLevel's defaults. Add your logo to the header by clicking the logo area and uploading your file.

Add Key Sections Now add supporting sections: a benefits section (3–4 bullet points explaining why you're different), a testimonial or social proof section, a services overview, and a final CTA section. Each section should move the visitor closer to conversion.

The builder works by dragging elements into place and editing in-line. Select an element, adjust its properties on the right. Want to move it? Drag it. Want to change the text? Click and type. It's fast and visual.

Mobile Optimization Toggle to the mobile view (usually a phone icon at the top) to see how your page looks on phones and tablets. GoHighLevel handles responsive design automatically, but check it. If a section looks cramped on mobile, adjust spacing or stack elements vertically.

Adding Pages, Navigation, and Site Structure

Your home page is live (in draft), but most sites need more. You likely need an About page, Services page, and Contact page at minimum.

Create New Pages At the top of the editor, find Pages or a pages panel. Click + Add Page. Name it (About, Services, Contact, etc.). GoHighLevel creates a new blank page. Build it the same way: add sections, text, images, forms.

Set Up Navigation Your navigation menu (the links at the top) needs to connect these pages. Edit your header section and click on the navigation menu. Add links pointing to each page you created. Most sites have: Home, About, Services, Contact. Keep it simple—five menu items max. Simplicity converts better.

Add Internal Links Within your pages, link to each other logically. If you mention a service on your home page, link it to your Services page. These internal links help with user experience and SEO.

Contact and Forms On your Contact page, add a form. GoHighLevel's form builder integrates directly with your CRM. When someone submits a form, they become a contact in your GoHighLevel account automatically. This is powerful—your website feeds your sales pipeline.

💡 Pro Tip

Add a clear, visible CTA button to every page. Don't make visitors hunt for how to contact you or take next steps. Your goal is friction-free conversion.

Publishing Your Website and Going Live

You've built your pages. Your site looks good. Now it's time to publish.

Review Everything First Before publishing, do a full review. Check spelling and grammar on every page. Test your forms—submit one, confirm the contact shows up in your GoHighLevel CRM. Click every link and button. Click your CTAs and make sure they go where they should. Test on mobile and desktop. A published site with broken links looks unprofessional.

Configure Your Domain GoHighLevel gives you a default subdomain (yourbusiness.gohighlevel.com). Most businesses use their own domain instead. Point your domain's DNS records to GoHighLevel's servers. GoHighLevel provides clear instructions for this in the website settings. The process takes 10 minutes and is one-time only.

Set Basic SEO GoHighLevel lets you add a page title and meta description for each page. These appear in Google search results. For your home page, use your main keyword (e.g., "Digital Marketing Agency in Austin") in the title. Write a 150-character meta description that summarizes what visitors will find. This small step improves click-through rates from search results.

Publish Find the Publish or Save & Publish button in the top-right corner of the editor. Click it. Your site goes live immediately. Visitors can now access it at your domain.

Post-Launch Publishing isn't the end—it's the beginning. Monitor your site for the first week. Check your analytics (GoHighLevel includes basic analytics). Are people finding you? Are they clicking your CTAs? Are forms converting? If not, adjust headlines, images, or CTAs based on what you learn.

Wrapping Up

Launching a website from blank in GoHighLevel is straightforward: plan first, build with intention, review thoroughly, and publish. You're not dealing with multiple platforms. You're not waiting for developers. You're not paying thousands of dollars. You're building a professional site yourself, integrated with your CRM and marketing tools, in record time.

The real power of GoHighLevel isn't just the website builder—it's that your website feeds directly into your business systems. When someone fills out a contact form, they're instantly in your CRM. You can email them. You can track their behavior. You can automate follow-up sequences. Your website becomes a lead generation machine, not just a digital business card.

If you're running an agency, managing multiple client sites, or building your own online presence, GoHighLevel removes the complexity. One platform. One login. Full control.
